quick_wins: Delete 432 stale branches across 129 projects — zero risk, instant cleanup
Add justification for 1 projects with ≤5 unjustified false positives each — quick audit fix

ai_insights.narrative: The SonarQube portfolio of 198 repositories is broadly healthy, though the most material hygiene risks are concentrated in inactive projects and stale non-main branches. 56 repositories have not been scanned for over a year, and a large number carry inactive non-main branches that inflate branch noise. Configuration gaps affect 11 repositories and four use a non-compliant Quality Gate, both of which weaken metric visibility and assurance consistency. Addressing the stale projects, cleaning up dormant branches, and restoring scanner configuration would meaningfully improve auditability across the portfolio.

sections.inactive_insights: Removing unused repositories can help to reduce unnecessary cost and maintenance effort. 56 repositories have not had a Sonar scan for more than 1 year, 46 of these for more than 2 years, and 27 for more than 4 years. These should be reviewed to check if they are still needed.
sections.inactive_action: Request project POCs to review these repositories for continued relevance, with inactive ones to be removed or archived from the SonarScan instance to optimise cost and maintain a clear project portfolio.
sections.duplication_insights: 1 repository exceeds the defined duplication threshold, indicating a higher level of repeated code.
sections.duplication_action: Review this repository with the project POC to identify and reduce duplication, and improve code efficiency and maintainability.
sections.branch_repos_with_inactive_non_main_bran: 129 repositories have non-main branches (e.g. feature or development branches) that have not been scanned for a prolonged period, which may indicate stale or abandoned work.
Review these repositories with project POCs to confirm if the branches are still needed, and remove or clean them up to maintain a well-managed Sonar environment.
sections.branch_repos_with_non_main_branch_count_: 9 repositories have more non-main branches than the agreed threshold, which can clutter scan results and make reporting harder to follow.
Review these repositories with project POCs to consolidate or remove surplus non-main branches and keep the branch count within the agreed threshold.
sections.false_positives_insights: 5 repositories have issues marked as false positives, and 1 of these has been recorded without a justification comment, weakening auditability.
sections.false_positives_action: Review the false-positive markings to confirm they are valid, and add a justification comment to the 1 repository missing one so the decision remains auditable.
sections.suppressions_insights: 3 repositories have accepted (suppressed) issues, and all 3 have been recorded without justification comments, weakening auditability.
sections.suppressions_action: Add justification comments to the accepted issues in the 3 repositories so suppression decisions remain auditable.
sections.quality_gates_insights: 4 repositories use a non-compliant Quality Gate, reducing assurance consistency across the portfolio.
sections.quality_gates_action: Restore the default Quality Gate on the 4 repositories using a non-compliant gate.
sections.config_gaps_insights: 11 repositories have scanner configuration gaps that suppress metric collection and reduce reporting visibility.
sections.config_gaps_action: Add the missing scanner configuration to the 11 repositories to restore metric visibility.
sections.sonarqube_rules_insights: 5 custom quality profiles do not inherit from a built-in profile, meaning they require manual updates and may drift from current rule standards. Two of these profiles have not been updated for over 800 days.
sections.sonarqube_rules_action: Make these custom profiles inherit from a built-in profile, or document why each must remain standalone, and review the long-stale profiles for continued relevance.
